en.extract:
The Cameroon olive greenbul (Phyllastrephus poensis), is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae.
It is found in the Cameroon line (including Bioko). Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. Alternate names for the Cameroon olive greenbul include Bioko greenbul and Cameroon olive bulbul.

The '''Cameroon olive greenbul''' ('''''Phyllastrephus poensis'''''), is a species of [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] family, [[Pycnonotidae]].<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Thelwall|first=Mike|date=2011-07-07|title=A comparison of link and URL citation counting|journal=ASLIB Proceedings|volume=63|issue=4|pages=419–425|doi=10.1108/00012531111148985|issn=0001-253X}}</ref>

It is found in the [[Cameroon line]] (including [[Bioko]]). Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. Alternate names for the Cameroon olive greenbul include '''Bioko greenbul''' and '''Cameroon olive bulbul'''.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-ūeoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=B6EA8FA2CB497B9A|title=Phyllastrephus poensis - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2017-05-04}}</ref>
